info.title: MICROSERVICE_DEV_Domestic Transfer Management API
info.description: The Domestic Transfer Management API facilitates efficient management of domestic money transfers between users. This service allows consumers to initiate, track, and manage their transfer requests seamlessly. Typical users include banks and financial institutions looking to streamline their clients' transfer processes. The API supports functionalities such as submitting transfer requests and monitoring their status, ensuring that users have real-time updates on their transactions.

post.description: This endpoint allows users to initiate a transfer of funds within the same country. Ensure that all necessary information such as the sender, recipient, and amount are provided in the request body to successfully complete the transaction.

requestBody.description: This request body is for initiating a domestic transfer operation. It should contain all necessary details regarding the transaction, such as sender and receiver information, amount, and any relevant identifiers.

500.description: An unexpected error occurred on the server. Please try again later.

404.description: The requested resource does not exist. Check the URL and try again.

400.description: The request was malformed or contained invalid parameters. Review the request data.

200.description: The request was successful and the response contains the requested data.

MICROSERVICE_DEV_createDomesticTransfer-.title: Domestic Transfer Error Response

timestamp.description: The time when the error occurred, represented as an ISO 8601 string.

status.description: Indicates whether the request was successful or not.

error.description: A numerical code representing the type of error encountered.

errorCode.description: An application-specific error code providing further detail on the error.

message.description: A human-readable message providing further information about the error.
message.example: The fund transfer request contains invalid or missing information.

path.description: The path of the request that resulted in the error.

traceId.description: A unique identifier for the request, useful for tracing through logs.

validationErrors.description: A list of validation errors encountered during the processing of the request.

example.message: Transfer amount must be greater than zero.

request.description: The original request data that resulted in the error.

suggestion.description: A suggestion to the user on how to resolve the issue.
suggestion.example: Verify the beneficiary details, transfer amount, and currency before retrying the request.

MICROSERVICE_DEV_createDomesticTransfer-.description: Schema representing the response for a domestic transfer operation when an error occurs.

example.message: The fund transfer request contains invalid or missing information.

validationErrors.message: Transfer amount must be greater than zero.

example.suggestion: Verify the beneficiary details, transfer amount, and currency before retrying the request.

MICROSERVICE_DEV_createDomesticTransfer-.title: Validation Error

field.description: The name of the field that caused the validation error.

rejectedValue.description: The value that was rejected during validation.

message.description: A message explaining the validation error.

MICROSERVICE_DEV_createDomesticTransfer-.description: Represents a validation error that occurred during request processing.

MICROSERVICE_DEV_createDomesticTransfer-.title: Transfer Request

transferReference.description: Unique reference identifier for the transfer.

sourceAccount.description: Account ID from which the funds are to be transferred.

beneficiaryId.description: ID of the beneficiary to whom the funds are to be transferred.

MICROSERVICE_DEV_createDomesticTransfer-.description: Represents the request data for a domestic transfer operation.

message.description: A human-readable message describing the error.

traceId.description: A unique identifier for tracing the request through the system.

validationErrors.description: An array of validation error details for the request.

example.message: Transfer amount must be greater than zero.

suggestion.description: A suggestion to help the user resolve the error.
suggestion.example: Verify the beneficiary details, transfer amount, and currency before retrying the request.

example.message: The fund transfer request contains invalid or missing information.

example.suggestion: Verify the beneficiary details, transfer amount, and currency before retrying the request.

request.description: The original request that caused the error.

suggestion.description: Suggested actions to resolve the error.
suggestion.example: Verify the beneficiary details, transfer amount, and currency before retrying the request.

MICROSERVICE_DEV_createDomesticTransfer-.title: Notification Preferences

MICROSERVICE_DEV_createDomesticTransfer-.title: Domestic Transfer Response

timestamp.description: The timestamp indicating when the response was generated.

status.description: Status code of the response indicating success or failure.

error.description: Error code if any error occurred during the operation; otherwise 0.

errorCode.description: A specific error code associated with the response, if applicable.

message.example: The fund transfer request contains invalid or missing information.

path.description: The path of the request that generated this response.

traceId.description: A unique identifier for tracking the request through the system.

validationErrors.description: List of validation errors that may have occurred during the transfer request.

example.message: Transfer amount must be greater than zero.

suggestion.description: Suggestions for correcting the input if the operation failed.
suggestion.example: Verify the beneficiary details, transfer amount, and currency before retrying the request.

MICROSERVICE_DEV_createDomesticTransfer-.description: Response object for a successful domestic transfer operation.

example.message: The fund transfer request contains invalid or missing information.

validationErrors.message: Transfer amount must be greater than zero.
